assertions.title: store_facts persists embeddings alongside new facts

steps.expected: Test passes: after store_facts inserts a fact, fact_embeddings has a row with correct model name and 384-dim embedding
steps.observed: PASSED — fact_embeddings row found with model='all-MiniLM-L6-v2' and 384-dim embedding blob

steps.expected: Facts with persisted embeddings loaded from DB; facts without fall back to model encoding; empty facts return empty arrays; when all persisted, model.encode not called
steps.observed: All 3 tests PASSED: test_loads_persisted_embeddings, test_empty_facts, test_all_persisted_no_encoding
Exit code 0, output shows 'usage: distill.py backfill_embeddings [-h]' with options help

assertions.title: backfill generates embeddings for facts missing them

steps.expected: backfill_fact_embeddings finds facts without embeddings, encodes and inserts them; skips facts that already have embeddings; returns 0 when all present
steps.observed: Both tests PASSED: test_backfill_missing (count=1 for the one missing embedding, both facts have embeddings after), test_backfill_all_present (count=0 when all already have embeddings)

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed. All persistence-related tests in test_fact_embeddings.py pass (11/11 total). CLI backfill_embeddings --help exits with code 0. store_facts correctly persists embeddings, _load_existing_fact_embeddings uses persisted data with fallback encoding, and backfill correctly fills only missing embeddings.
